summary: iter55 — Verified: (1) Fondo Empresa outflow_orders reduces balance ONLY when order.status=completed AND delivery_method IN (transfer, cash, crypto). Accumulate & approved leave balance untouched. (2) /api/admin/transactions + /api/me/transactions emit ref_type='order_payout' rows for the same set (38 payout rows visible for admin, 5 for VIP). CSV export contains 'order_payout'. (3) Rate PUT triggers push fanout without crashing (200 OK on both delta and no-op updates). (4) Order status PUT->completed returns 200 and side-effect push does not throw. Backend: 17/17 iter55 pytest + 10/10 live-URL smoke + 28/28 company-funds regression = 55/55 PASS. Frontend: /admin/company-funds subtitle & red 'Entregado a clientes' + fund-order-out-CUP testid confirmed; /admin/transactions payout modal shows 'ID ORDEN', 'DATOS DEL BENEFICIARIO', 'COMPROBANTE DEL PAGO AL CLIENTE' + 'IR A ÓRDENES'; /dashboard/transactions VIP shows 'SALIDA CUP 2,370 TRANSFER COMPLETED' with modal 'COMPROBANTE DEL PAGO RECIBIDO'.

minor.issue: services/transactions.py:205 items.sort(key=lambda x: x.get('created_at','')) 500s with TypeError when any doc in scope has a numeric created_at (e.g. float from legacy or badly-seeded data) mixed with ISO strings. Defensive fix: coerce key to str(x.get('created_at') or '').

issues: Rendered box appears empty visually even though /api/files/order_payouts/... returns HTTP 200 — likely the seeded proof is a 1x1 PNG. Not a bug in prod flow, but consider adding an object-fit/min-height fallback so tiny images don't collapse to invisible strip.

action_items: (Minor) Harden services/transactions.py sort key against mixed-type created_at to prevent 500 if a legacy order has a non-string timestamp.
(Cosmetic) TransactionDetailModal: give the proof a min-height/border-fallback so a small/invalid image doesn't render as an invisible frame.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/admin_company_funds.py _compute_company_funds correctly adds outflow_orders only when status=='completed' AND delivery_method in {transfer,cash,crypto}; balance formula matches subtitle: inflow+manual_inflow − outflow_orders − outflow_clients − outflow_company − manual_outflow. Verified via CUP baseline arithmetic.
services/transactions.py build_transactions emits order_payout for the same gating, and includes proof_image + delivery_details in the payload — good for the modal.
services/transactions.py:205 the sort key defaults to '' which mixes types when created_at is numeric. Use str(x.get('created_at') or '') to avoid TypeError.
push_service.build_rate_changed_payload fanout is fire-and-forget (rate PUT stays 200 even if push subsystem misbehaves) — good design, best-effort as declared.
Frontend uses distinct testid prefixes: admin=tx-row-{id}, VIP dashboard=my-tx-row-{id}. Consistent modal testid tx-detail-modal on both sides.
test_credentials: Used /app/memory/test_credentials.md — admin cookie test_session_admin_X, VIP cookie test_session_vip_X, TOTP secret JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXPJBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP (passed inline in JSON body as totp_code, NOT as x-totp-code header).
seed_data_creation: Live smoke tests insert TEST_ITER41_* orders directly into db.orders and clean them via module-scoped fixture teardown (order_number regex match). Manual PATCH test also cleaned inline.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rate PUT endpoint requires totp_code IN the JSON BODY (not header) — model field TotpProtected. Order status transition uses PUT /api/admin/orders/{id}/status (NOT PATCH); requires payout_proof_image + payout_delivery_details when transitioning to 'completed' for transfer/cash/crypto orders. Transaction docs use ISO-string created_at everywhere — legacy float timestamps break the sort in services/transactions.py:205 (500). Frontend testid prefixes: admin=tx-row, VIP=my-tx-row.
rca of the issue: N/A — no regressions. Only new observation is the sort-key type-mix vulnerability in services/transactions.py, which is a defensive-hardening comment rather than a live bug (nothing in normal code paths writes non-string created_at).
